preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: growth-focused SaaS teams who need a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent
brief.goal: demonstrate MODE's ability to generate intent-aware pages at funnel scale

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for growth-focused SaaS teams, immediately framing MODE as a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent. Uses the with-price hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, anchoring the decision-stage visitor before proof sections follow.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validator archetype demands trust signals before evaluating claims. Presenting customer logos, partner endorsements, or industry recognition early satisfies their need for authority and legitimacy, reducing skepticism before deeper product content.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Articulates MODE's core capabilities — intent-aware page generation, semantic design tokens, funnel-scale automation — translating features into outcomes. Positioned after Credibility so the Validator reads capability claims through a lens of already-established trust.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for the decision stage and critical for the Validator archetype. Delivers hard proof — metrics on conversion lift, case study data from SaaS teams, before/after comparisons — that substantiates the Value beat's promises with verifiable results.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Frames plan comparison and pricing clearly so the visitor can evaluate commitment options. Placed after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized proof, making the pricing context feel justified rather than premature.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Final action prompt positioned immediately before Recovery per structural rules. For a decision-stage organic visitor, the CTA is direct — start a trial, schedule a demo, or select a plan — capitalizing on the proof-then-price narrative the Validator just experienced.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary navigation paths, footer links, and exit handling for visitors who aren't ready to commit. Ensures no dead end — offering documentation, a comparison guide, or a 'talk to sales' fallback that respects the Validator's need for additional due diligence.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context for growth-focused SaaS teams, immediately framing MODE as a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent. Uses the with-price hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, anchoring the decision-stage visitor before proof sections follow.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validator archetype demands trust signals before evaluating claims. Presenting customer logos, partner endorsements, or industry recognition early satisfies their need for authority and legitimacy, reducing skepticism before deeper product content.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Articulates MODE's core capabilities — intent-aware page generation, semantic design tokens, funnel-scale automation — translating features into outcomes. Positioned after Credibility so the Validator reads capability claims through a lens of already-established trust.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required for the decision stage and critical for the Validator archetype. Delivers hard proof — metrics on conversion lift, case study data from SaaS teams, before/after comparisons — that substantiates the Value beat's promises with verifiable results.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Frames plan comparison and pricing clearly so the visitor can evaluate commitment options. Placed after Evidence so the Validator has already internalized proof, making the pricing context feel justified rather than premature.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Final action prompt positioned immediately before Recovery per structural rules. For a decision-stage organic visitor, the CTA is direct — start a trial, schedule a demo, or select a plan — capitalizing on the proof-then-price narrative the Validator just experienced.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion context requires minimal variant — 'strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' — the persistent CTA is 'the most converted element on most SaaS pages' and should not compete with decision moment below.
Validator archetype in decision stage requires trust signals; 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster' to establish credibility at the critical conversion moment.

slots.headline: Your design system renders. MODE decides why.
slots.subhead: MODE adds a semantic resolution engine above your component library — so every page adapts to archetype, funnel stage, and intent without manual variants or QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands social proof with attribution; 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity' and 'always include role and company for Validator credibility.' Place near CTA as 'testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.'

slots.quote: We were building variant after variant by hand — Mover layout, Validator layout, Explorer layout. MODE collapsed that into a single brief. The resolution engine makes the right call before we even review it.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from 'specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements' and '3-col-icon is the default' for structured capability articulation at decision stage.

slots.headline: Everything that makes your design system context-aware
slots.subhead: Eleven components. Four resolution dimensions. One brief to full page architecture — without a single manual variant.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — automatically, on every render.

features.title: IA planner agent
features.description: Brief to full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, wired to your Anthropic key, zero vendor billing.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel — ready to evaluate, not ready to hand off.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage. No manual color assignment — the system accumulates weight semantically toward conversion.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Nothing ambiguous about where or why a component fires.

features.title: Full code ownership
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase on Radix UI primitives. No runtime dependency, no lock-in. Fork it, extend it, ship it on your terms.

page.reasoning: Validator context requires 'always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust' to anchor commitment credibility.

stats.label: Median time from install to first intent-resolved page in production
Archetype-differentiated page variants generated from a single brief, in parallel
External dependencies added to your production bundle — MODE is configuration, not infrastructure

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires clear offer anchor; 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' with Validator enrichment including 'description and longer feature list' to support the commitment decision.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, component manifest, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription. No seat limits. No lock-in.
features: Semantic resolution engine — four dimension model
Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Bring your own Anthropic API key

page.reasoning: Validator decision stage requires 'include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' to reduce commitment friction and reinforce credibility at the conversion moment.

slots.headline: Ready to stop building variants by hand?
slots.subhead: MODE is running in under an hour. Configure once — the resolution engine handles the rest.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription required.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion page benefits from 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' to maximize final conversion opportunity.
